risk_title: Weak institutional capacity and governance constraints in decentralized health structures
risk_summary: District Health Management Teams (DHMTs) lack adequate systems, capacity, and accountability mechanisms for managing project resources and processes, limiting IHPAU's confidence in decentralizing funds and decisions. This governance fragility is hindering project implementation speed.
evidence_quote: Limited capacity of DHMT personnel and structures also limits the extent to which IHPAU wishes to confidently decentralize decisions, funds and responsibilities to DHMTs given risks of potential ineligible expenditures.

risk_title: Lack of technical coordination and oversight at Ministry of Health level
risk_summary: Despite high-level political support, the project has suffered from persistent lack of technical coordination and oversight at MoH level, evidenced by non-convening of required Steering Committee Meetings. This governance gap is affecting project implementation continuity.
evidence_quote: the project has continuously suffered from lack of technical coordination and oversight at MoH level. This has been documented in the two most recent missions, evidenced by the fact that the Steering Committee Meetings outlined in the project's Financial Agreements have not taken place.

risk_title: Systemic health system governance and financing constraints
risk_summary: Sierra Leone's health system is constrained by serious structural governance issues, very limited government health spending (less than 5% of government budget), and constrained fiscal space. These systemic governance and financing weaknesses directly affect project implementation effectiveness.
evidence_quote: The health system in Sierra Leone is constrained by serious structural issues across all health system pillars, which in turn affect effective and efficient project implementation. Challenges are particularly salient on...health financing with very limited government spending for the health sector (less than 5 percent of government spending) and a constrained fiscal space for resource mobilization, and governance.

risk_title: Delayed implementation of Grievance Redress Mechanism affecting project legitimacy
risk_summary: Persistent delays in establishing a functional Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM) have resulted in downgrading of Environmental and Social Standards rating to Moderately Unsatisfactory. Grievances are currently being documented manually but not reported, undermining accountability and community trust.
evidence_quote: the project is currently documenting, and tracking grievances manually but these are not being reported...the mission noted with concern the continued delays in actualizing the implementation of the approved GRM roadmap. These delays resulted in the downgrading of the E&S rating from Moderately Satisfactory to Moderately Unsatisfactory.

risk_title: Weak partnership implementation capacity with international NGO
risk_summary: Partners in Health (PIH) lacks prior experience executing World Bank IPF projects and undertaking public procurement processes under WB rules. This capacity gap in the implementing partner is affecting project execution speed and quality.
evidence_quote: prior to QEHSSSP, PIH had no experience in executing project under WB rules and regulations for IPF projects nor undertaking public procurement processes.
